steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both glossy ibises and little blue herons are drawn to areas with standing water for more than 60 days per year. The question asks where neither species is very drawn, so the correct choice must describe habitat that inverts this shared preference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — there is relatively deep water during the breeding season.: focuses on one species, not a condition that excludes both. ✗
  • Choice B — there are any features that attract the other species.: too imprecise and not supported by the data in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — species with breeding seasons longer than 60 days are likely to be present.: misapplies the 60-day figure, which refers to standing water, not breeding duration. ✗
  • Choice D — there is standing water for far fewer than 60 days per year.: directly inverts both species' shared requirement, identifying exactly where neither would be attracted. ✓
D is correct.
